data.title: Principal Financial Group raises dividend by 2.5% to $0.82
data.description:
• Principal Financial Group (PFG) declares $0.82/share quarterly dividend, 2.5% increase from prior dividend of $0.80.
  • Forward yield 3.3%
  • Payable June 26; for shareholders of record June 1; ex-div June 1.
  • See PFG Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Wall Street mixed as Israel-Lebanon truce gets extended
data.description:
The stock market opened mixed on Friday as the ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon got extended by three weeks. 

The S&P 500 (SP500) +0.2%, the Dow (DJI) -0.3%, and the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) +0.6%. 

President Donald Trump said in a social media post that he had ordered the U.S. Navy to “shoot and kill any boat” caught laying mines in the strait.

Treasury yields edged slightly lower across the curve. The 2-year yield (US2Y) dipped to 3.84%, while the 10-year yield (US10Y) eased to 4.33%. The 30-year yield (US30Y) also inched down to 4.91%.

The economic calendar for the day includes the consumer sentiment report.

data.title: Pony AI gains after updating on its robotaxi progress
data.description:
Pony AI (PONY) used the Auto China 2026 conference in Beijing to highlight the company's stronger push toward large-scale commercialization of its autonomous driving business, led by lower-cost Gen-7 robotaxis, fleet expansion, and a more efficient AI training system. Notably, the company highlighted that the total cost of its 2027 robotaxi in China, including the base vehicle and autonomous driving kit, is expected to fall below RMB230K while its fleet has grown to 1,400 vehicles from 270 and user registrations have surpassed 1M.

In terms of profitability, the company noted that its Gen-7 kit has delivered a 70% reduction in bill-of-materials costs, helping it reach unit-economics breakeven in two major Southern China markets. Pony AI (PONY) said it plans to expand the fleet to more than 3K robotaxis by the end of this year and grow to 20 cities globally, with nearly half overseas, while also preparing an international version of its Gen-7 platform for local market requirements.

"Today, the question is no longer whether Robotaxi can work. The focus is on how to scale it safely, efficiently, and at the right cost," updated Pony AI (PONY) CEO Dr. James Peng.

Shares of Pony AI (PONY) were up 2% in premarket action.

data.title: Merck acquisition of Terns passes HSR waiting period expiration
data.description:
• Merck's (MRK) $6.7B deal to acquire Terns Pharmaceuticals (TERN), announced in March, has made it through the expiration of the Hart-Scott-Rodino Act, signaling there are no antitrust concerns with the transaction from regulators.
  • The deal is a cash tender with Merck purchasing all outstanding shares of common stock of Terns for $53.00 each.
  • Closing is expected in Q2.

data.title: Japan CPI climbs to 1.5% as Yen weakens: Here are 10 best performing Japanese ADRs in 2026
data.description:
Japan's equity markets edged higher on Friday despite fresh inflation data that underscored the delicate balancing act facing the Bank of Japan.

The Nikkei 225 rose 0.6% toward 59,500 while the broader Topix gained 0.4% to 3,730, even as the Japanese yen weakened toward 160 per U.S. dollar, on track for a nearly 1% weekly decline, raising concerns about import-driven price pressures down the line.

March CPI data released Friday showed annual inflation rising to 1.5%, up from 1.3% in February, with the monthly CPI jumping 0.4% to its highest level since January 2025. 

Against this macro backdrop of modest but building inflationary pressure and a weakening yen, the following list ranks the top 10 U.S.-listed Japanese ADRs by their year-to-date performance, offering a snapshot of which Japanese companies have held up best for international investors through the first four months of 2026.

SoftBank Corp. (SOBKY) leads the list with a YTD gain of 0.15%, followed by Coca-Cola Bottlers Japan Holdings (CCOJY) at 0.39% and SECOM (SOMLY) at 0.79%, a tight cluster at the top that reflects the broadly muted returns Japanese equities have delivered in U.S. dollar terms as yen weakness erodes offshore gains.

Fanuc Corporation (FANUY) and Japan Tobacco (JAPAY) round out the top five with gains of 1.13% and 1.23%, the only two names on the list carrying Quant Ratings, both Holds. KOSE Holdings (KSRYY) posts the strongest absolute YTD return on the list at 2.81%, reflecting resilience in Japan's premium beauty and cosmetics segment, while Sanrio (SNROY) and Obayashi (OBYCF) follow closely.

Here is the list:

  1. SoftBank Corp. (SOBKY), YTD performance: 0.15%

  2. Coca-Cola Bottlers Japan Holdings Inc. (CCOJY), YTD performance: 0.39%

  3. SECOM CO., LTD. (SOMLY), YTD performance: 0.79%

  4. Fanuc Corporation (FANUY), YTD performance: 1.13%

  5. Japan Tobacco Inc. (JAPAY), YTD performance: 1.23%

  6. Digital Garage, Inc. (DLGEF), YTD performance: 1.33%

  7. Toyo Suisan Kaisha, Ltd. (TSUKY), YTD performance: 2.45%

  8. Obayashi Corporation (OBYCF), YTD performance: 2.65%

  9. Sanrio Company, Ltd. (SNROY), YTD performance: 2.74%

  10. KOSE Holdings Corporation (KSRYY), YTD performance: 2.81%
